news.title:
Telecom sector faces higher opex due to diesel price hike by ₹22/litre for industrial users.
Major diesel consumer Indus Towers and others may experience cost impacts from bulk price surge.
news.summary:
Telecom sector faces higher opex due to diesel price hike by ₹22/litre for industrial users.
Major diesel consumer Indus Towers and others may experience cost impacts from bulk price surge.

news.title:
Emkay Global revises portfolio, adding Infosys, HCL Tech, Indus Towers, SRF; exits Mphasis, Sun Pharma, Gravita.
It turns marginally overweight on IT sector, citing attractive valuations post AI sell-off; sees 6.5-18.3% return potential.
news.summary:
Emkay Global revises portfolio, adding Infosys, HCL Tech, Indus Towers, SRF; exits Mphasis, Sun Pharma, Gravita.
It turns marginally overweight on IT sector, citing attractive valuations post AI sell-off; sees 6.5-18.3% return potential.

Indus Towers witnessed high trading volume on Monday due to promoter buying.
Precision Wires hit its 52-week high during the session.

news.title:
Citi identifies three near-term catalysts for Indus Towers.
Citi maintains a target price of 540 for Indus Towers.
news.summary:
Citi identifies three near-term catalysts for Indus Towers.
Citi maintains a target price of 540 for Indus Towers.

news.title:
Indus Towers' Q3 revenue grew 7.9% YoY to INR 81.5B; core rental revenue rose 9.5%.
3,548 towers and 6,105 colocation sites added; EBITDA margin at 55.3% with slight YoY decline.
news.summary:
Indus Towers' Q3 revenue grew 7.9% YoY to INR 81.5B; core rental revenue rose 9.5%.
3,548 towers and 6,105 colocation sites added; EBITDA margin at 55.3% with slight YoY decline.

news.title:
Indus Towers supports India's telecom growth with 1.21B subscribers.
Rural tele density rises to 59.4; Urban tele density drives engagement.
news.summary:
Indus Towers supports India's telecom growth with 1.21B subscribers.
Rural tele density rises to 59.4; Urban tele density drives engagement.

news.title:
Indus Towers reports 7.9% revenue growth, driven by strategic 5G initiatives.
The company plans further expansion into African markets to bolster growth.
news.summary:
Indus Towers reports 7.9% revenue growth, driven by strategic 5G initiatives.
The company plans further expansion into African markets to bolster growth.

news.title:
Indus Towers Ltd reported audited financial results for Q3 and nine months ended Dec 31, 2025, with revenue at Rs 81,463 million.
Net profit for the period stood at Rs 17,759 million, with comprehensive income at Rs 17,759 million. The board approved results on Feb 2, 2026.
news.summary:
Indus Towers Ltd reported audited financial results for Q3 and nine months ended Dec 31, 2025, with revenue at Rs 81,463 million.
Net profit for the period stood at Rs 17,759 million, with comprehensive income at Rs 17,759 million. The board approved results on Feb 2, 2026.

news.title:
75 companies, including Tata Chemicals, PB Fintech, and Mahindra Lifespace, will announce Q3FY26 results on Feb. 2.
Hyundai's Q2FY26 profit rose 14.3% YoY, while Bajaj Housing Finance's profit grew 18% YoY; Indus Towers' profit fell 17.3%.
news.summary:
75 companies, including Tata Chemicals, PB Fintech, and Mahindra Lifespace, will announce Q3FY26 results on Feb. 2.
Hyundai's Q2FY26 profit rose 14.3% YoY, while Bajaj Housing Finance's profit grew 18% YoY; Indus Towers' profit fell 17.3%.

news.title:
Vodafone Idea shares surged 14% after AGR relief bolstered its 3-year investment strategy. Citi sees cash flow support aiding a ₹25,000 crore debt raise.
Citi flagged ambitious growth targets and tariff hikes as key factors. Indus Towers' prospects improve, with a Buy rating and ₹515 price target.
news.summary:
Vodafone Idea shares surged 14% after AGR relief bolstered its 3-year investment strategy. Citi sees cash flow support aiding a ₹25,000 crore debt raise.
Citi flagged ambitious growth targets and tariff hikes as key factors. Indus Towers' prospects improve, with a Buy rating and ₹515 price target.

The subsidiary will act as investment holding and treasury entity with Rs 20 crore initial investment.

The new entity aims to expand its telecom infrastructure business internationally.

The entities aim to establish telecom infrastructure; initial share capital funded in cash.
